articles.title: CONGRESSMAN BEGICH'S BITCOIN RESERVE PLAN
articles.summary: Rep. Nick Begich is pushing the BITCOIN Act, a bill co-sponsored with Sen. Cynthia Lummis that would make Bitcoin a strategic reserve asset and direct the US to acquire one million BTC over five years using budget-neutral strategies, citing its role in securing the country's economic future. America's balance sheet is overly weighted toward gold and should emphasize Bitcoin. The proposal, which builds on Trump's executive order protecting seized Bitcoin from liquidation, has gained support from crypto leaders including Michael Saylor.

articles.title: SUI GROUP TO LAUNCH TWO STABLECOINS
articles.summary: Nasdaq-listed Sui Group plans to issue two stablecoins on the Sui blockchain in partnership with Ethena (suiUSDe, which offers yield, and USDi, which does not). The launch, expected by year-end, adds to growing competition among dollar-pegged tokens.

articles.title: STARKNET INTRODUCES BITCOIN STAKING
articles.summary: Starknet has launched BTC staking via wrapped assets like WBTC, tBTC, and Liquid Bitcoin, allowing holders to earn rewards while keeping custody and helping secure its L2 network. The Starknet Foundation is supporting the rollout with 100M STRK ($12M) in incentives to grow BTCFi, including borrowing and yield strategies, while Re7 Capital will introduce an institutional-grade BTC yield product in October.

articles.title: USDC EXPLORING REVERSIBLE TRANSACTIONS
articles.summary: Circle is examining mechanisms to allow USDC transaction reversals in cases of fraud or hacks while maintaining settlement finality, creating tension between immediate transfers and irrevocability that challenges crypto's foundational principle of permanent, immutable transactions. The exploration of reversibility aims to help scam victims and bolster mainstream trust in stablecoins, though it counters the decentralized model where transactions remain immune from unilateral changes by issuers or validators, with supporters pointing to successful examples like Sui validators freezing and returning $162 million during the Cetus exploit. Circle president Heath Tarbert argues that while blockchain technology may be superior, traditional finance systems offer benefits not currently present in crypto, suggesting developers recognize the need for some reversibility, provided all parties agree.

articles.title: CRYPTO'S LODESTAR: FUNDAMENTALS
articles.summary: Despite Bitcoin near all-time highs, nihilistic sentiment pervades crypto as people recognize predatory market structures, including memecoins that reward influence over insight, large-cap supply games with high-FDV low-float launches and structured market-making deals, and retail-as-exit tokenomics where projects lacking revenue paths treat tokens as the product. The "revenue meta" represents a structural shift where speculative premiums compress as wealth rotates from outsiders to insiders, pushing prices toward fundamental floors with revenue becoming the baseline for sustainable valuations, similar to traditional finance, where profitable firms buy back shares when return on equity outpaces internal rate of return. Crypto's infrastructure advantages enable unprecedented scaling with Tether and Hyperliquid achieving over $100 million revenue per employee, while internet-native distribution through social platforms allows products to reach 100 million users in weeks, AI-accelerated building enables solo founders to create serious businesses, and programmable incentives reward users, creators, and referrers natively. Internet Capital Markets represent the inevitable evolution toward a single global layer with native issuance, trading, and settlement of any asset accessible worldwide, potentially creating millions of investable internet-native businesses onchain as startup costs decrease and scaling speed increases.

articles.title: MELEE MARKETS: PERMISSIONLESS PREDICTION MARKETS
articles.summary: Melee Markets addresses the limitations of existing centralized prediction market platforms that restrict market creation and rely on market makers for liquidity, typically optimizing only for narrow events like elections or major sports outcomes rather than capturing the full spectrum of cultural conversation from breaking news to everyday opinions. The platform introduces "Viral Markets," a new primitive that allows creators to launch questions of any kind – factual or opinion-based – while users speculate through a pricing mechanism that rewards early and accurate predictions, with speculators gaining exposure to both the outcome and the market's growth itself. This design combines prediction market finality with token-like asymmetric upside, solving the cold start problem that has constrained the category while offering creators a sustainable way to monetize influence without reputational risk. At scale, Melee envisions a permissionless network where creators, speculators, and consumers crowdsource truth without gatekeepers, positioning prediction markets as a burgeoning category similar to social networks where multiple winners can emerge by distinguishing themselves across different dimensions and vertical niches.

articles.title: FIVE POINTS AGAINST TOM LEE'S ETH THESIS
articles.summary: Tom Lee's Ethereum thesis is dismantled across five key arguments, starting with the stablecoin and RWA adoption claim, where despite 100-1000x increases in tokenized asset value and stablecoin volumes since 2020, ETH transaction fees remain at 2020 levels due to network efficiency upgrades, activity migrating to other chains, and low-velocity tokenized assets generating minimal fees. The critique challenges Lee's "digital oil" comparison by noting that oil has traded in the same inflation-adjusted range for over a century with periodic spikes that revert, making commodity status bearish rather than bullish for ETH. The institutional adoption argument falls flat as no major banks have purchased ETH for balance sheets or announced plans to do so, with analysts noting that banks don't stockpile gasoline or buy shares in custodians they use. Lee's claim that ETH will equal the value of all financial infrastructure companies reflects fundamental misunderstanding of value accrual, while his technical analysis cherry-picks data to support bias rather than objectively showing Ethereum in a multi-year range similar to crude oil, with recent resistance failures and bearish technicals suggesting potential for extended $1,000-$4,800 trading range driven by saturated narratives and fundamentals that don't justify valuation growth.

articles.title: THE STABLECOIN DUOPOLY FACES REAL COMPETITION
articles.summary: Tether and Circle's combined 85% market share is slipping as new issuers, regulatory clarity under the GENIUS Act, and yield-sharing models reshape the landscape. Fintechs, exchanges, DeFi protocols, and wallets now have incentives to launch their own stables or strike revenue share deals, capturing float revenue that USDT and USDC traditionally hoarded. Emerging contenders like Ethena's USDe, Ondo's USDY, PayPal's PYUSD, and bank-issued consortium stables point to a more fragmented market, where frictionless swaps and homogenized regulation erode network effects. Banks' eventual entry could dwarf today's $300B market, making the next phase of stablecoins far more competitive than the Tether/Circle era.

articles.title: COINBASE APP TAPS 1INCH FOR DEFI SWAPS
articles.summary: Coinbase is integrating 1inch's Swap API to power non-custodial token swaps, marking 1inch's largest US client to date.

articles.title: THE L1 PREMIUM DEBATE
articles.summary: Many projects choose to launch as Layer 1s instead of Layer 2s because of the “L1 Premium” (inflated valuations given simply for being an L1).

articles.title: COINBASE'S SEPTEMBER HIGHLIGHTS
articles.summary: Coinbase rolled out DeFi lending with up to 7.4% yields on USDC, listed new stablecoins ($AUDD, $XSGD), and partnered with Google to power AI agent payments via x402.

articles.title: AERODROME POWERS BASE ECONOMY
articles.summary: Aerodrome generated over $250M in Chain GDP over the past year, making up more than 30% of Base's total economy and outpacing protocols like Uniswap, Aave, and Virtuals.
